payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: If Shakäste comes back later on, you can play his spiritual weapon.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Wait, leave it on, because you kind of look like Estelle Getty. (laughter)

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: --to acquire a wax seal within the home of Lord Sutan to create a letter that you feel would be incriminating, then to infiltrate the High-Richter's home, do the same, and then report them to the lawmaster, or whoever else you'd think would be of higher estate. This is transpiring tomorrow evening. Where we last left off, it was later in the afternoon after your final discussion. You guys have a day to prepare however you wish to.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Hello and welcome back. Last we left off, the Mighty Nein had gotten in bed with a rebel group who had been meeting in the interior of Zadash. Unhappy with some members of the local government of this city, they had been trying to discover a way they could discredit some of the corrupt individuals that have been causing some strife in this city to some of the locals. You had met some of these individuals a few times to discover that there is an End of Harvest Gala that was arriving fairly soon. That was going to be a distraction period to allow them to infiltrate the abodes of two individuals in particular: Lord Sutan and the High-Richter Prucine. You discussed with them last time a plot, fixing their not-very-well-put-together plot into a better direction, thankfully.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We're all fucked.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All right. You guys make your way, following Nott, who had previously trailed Dolan to the home, and you eventually find your way to the outskirts of a very simple-- it’s a hovel, but only because it's been in disuse for a while. There is a single front door, it looks to be a two-chamber home, with one large central main living space and one bedroom in the back. The roof itself is made of heavily pressed-together thatch and stick. As soon as you approach, you can see that the front door is closed, and you see a little bit of movement through the window, someone walking around.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That seems like a good idea.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: One thing at a time.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That's another thing-- and we can table this-- but there is also the possibility that this is all just a honey trap, and that we’re being drawn in for as big a fish than the Knights are themselves.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You think we're big fish?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, I think that they think they are looking for potentially big fish. I'm not saying that we are big fish. But we might be walking into a trap, so eyes on a swivel, is all I’m saying.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: So you're saying Ulog probably is in trouble and is going to roll over on us in order to get something taken off of him.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Potentially, or any of them. Ulog seems the shiftiest, but that doesn't mean that he would necessarily be--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Ulog’s probably listening to us right now.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: To Dolan's, then.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All of us? All right, let's go.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You got maybe an hour and half to two hours before sunset.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I will go to Sutan's.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Wait, they're both Sutan.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: No, the elder, I'm sorry.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Will you be able to get in?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Not without you. You'll come with me?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We'll sneak on in through the sewers?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Well how do you want to do it?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: They're talking about going straight in through the gate because they're, you know, respectable.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (distraught) Well it's such a thing to get into the sewers. We have to make a distraction and everything, every time.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Perhaps we should just have a two person reconnaissance for now.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We can manage ourselves.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We're good.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yes, we won't get into any trouble.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, you won't bring back Clarota or nothing, it'll be great.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We know our way around.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Too soon.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Can't be.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We got this, let's go.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Should we meet back at the Song & Supper Inn at what, sunset?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What time is it?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Or just at the Leaky Tap.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Okay, same difference. So you manage, through asking questions and finding your way around, you manage to get to the Tutor Village right around sunset. You do see, about one block over, the large, white-peach building that you had previously been told by Dolan. You see the sign up; it's called the Guided Hand Infirmary. It's one story, but it stretches wide towards the back. It's like a large shotgun building.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: No, we're going to break into houses together. Of course I'm willing to do this. I just feel like for this-- what are you planning to do?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Just some simple reconnaissance. See if we can see Ren, maybe have a conversation with him. If he's not a total prick, I don't know, maybe he's got some of his dad's handwriting.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We don't have to go, I don't care.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: No it's fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It sounds scary to me.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: No I-- my head is not in the game. We'll go along.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Great, we're going to head towards the Apple Tree Tuneship? What did I write here?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) The Tutoring Village.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Thank you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make an investigation check.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh god. Nine, twelve.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Twelve, okay. It takes you a little while to get there. You haven't actually been to the Tutor Village before.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: As Frumpkin keeps watch on each of these, each chamber seems to either be empty or there's somebody in some state of either rest, or waiting. You see one person, a younger girl maybe fifteen or sixteen years old, who is waiting on the edge of a bed, sitting quietly in a room by herself. Glancing around, she looks over and sees Frumpkin, and goes, "Kitty!" and makes the hand motion to try and pet-- You go around the back door and you can see one room has two beds that currently have two individuals that are coughing heavily. They're sick with something pretty bad, and there is a person inside that is dressed in a white robe that is in the process of bringing them both broth, but they have a mask over their face, a piece of cloth tied behind, and it looks like they're treating some diseased patients. This is, for all intents and purposes, an infirmary.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Seniors '99! (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Wooo! Sydney football rules. Weird. But, you do see this individual is currently has a full arm bandaged, with blood soaked and dried on parts of it, and they are currently asleep, and the door is closed. There is a lantern inside that is set to keep the room warm.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Is there the ability for Frumpkin to move around to the exact opposite side of this building?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, have him move around, go around the front.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, he will jump up to any windowsill on the back of the house as well.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: As you're focusing, Fjord, you notice a small child, maybe five or six years old, this scruffy reddish hair and a dirty smashed face, walks up and goes, "What happened to the kitty?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: One last thing, how high is the roof?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The roof is about, I'd say, 12 feet up.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Fjord, come here. Scooch down for a second, and I jump up onto-- I've taken my vision back-- I jump up onto his back. Now, stand up!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: 11 strength. Okay… Oof!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay. I snap at Frumpkin, and he vanishes, and I reach out, and Frumpkin appears. I am now within ten feet, I would assume, of the top of the building, so Frumpkin is on the roof. (teleportation noise) I want to send him to look-- Then I hop down off of Ford and put my hand and steady myself on Fjord and have Frumpkin look for any holes in the roof, or if there's a skylight, or anything. Probably not, but it's worth a try.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: It would take you about an hour or so to do so. To a studied physician, probably not going to hold up.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I mean, we've got Mollymauk with us. Why don't you just do the same thing to him?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I could kick the shit out of Nott in the face, and then like--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I don't appreciate this. At all. I feel like I'm being singled out because I'm a woman. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: A better idea, perhaps, is for one of-- You're able to disguise yourself, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: To a point.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: There's three of us, that we can all disguise ourselves. I can make myself look like like a sick person.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Although, he's a physician. So he might try to, you know, check. And I think, up to touch, it falls apart.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, you can make yourself look like you, but with not green skin.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's standard carny practice to be able to fake boils and stuff with a disguise kit to leper up a bit?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: That's up to you, you can try and duck into a shady corner. And spend an hour.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: How long do you need to make your face all lepered up?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: About an hour?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: About an hour.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: And who said I was going to do my face?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Couldn't you maybe act like--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We don't want them to look at it too closely.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Look like you're just-- Cough and stuff?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Could you be a sick tiefling?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I'm going to do that, too. I've got a plan.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You’re going to spend time and apply.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I've got it, I've got it. Faster. What if Caleb burns all of your clothing and you go in as a burn victim?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You're not going to touch my clothing.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That's not, we’re not doing that.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: No, we're not setting fire to my clothing. I'm going to mess up my junk what fierce.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh my god.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Gross.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: In the street?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: No, back at the-- do we have a place where I can work, or am I going back to the Leaky Tap? Or is that too far away?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: 11. Okay. You take an hour and you're pretty proud of it. You think it'll work fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: In your butt.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. If you're gathering all those materials from a scattered meal--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: A little bit of hot sauce.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: We'll put it at about eight copper for the total.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'll pay for it. Don't. It's on me.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: No problem. We're going to put it in my-- I've got a little water sack. I'm going to mix it all up and put it in there and wait till we're ready to go.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. I'm going to say for this, go ahead and roll a d20. Add your proficiency bonus and intelligence modifier.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Come on, stage makeup class!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: D20, proficiency bonus, intelligence modifier?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Because this is about trying to come up with an accurate means of looking like you've been infected with something.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That's 11.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: For a month.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. I'm going to say for this, go ahead and roll a d20. Add your proficiency bonus and intelligence modifier.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Come on, stage makeup class!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: D20, proficiency bonus, intelligence modifier?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Because this is about trying to come up with an accurate means of looking like you've been infected with something.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That's 11.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 11. Okay. You take an hour and you're pretty proud of it. You think it'll work fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Did you add your intelligence bonus?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I have no intelligence bonus.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, great.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Do I dare ask what it looks like?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You can see.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, show us the goods. What did you do to yourself? Oh!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Why would you even?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That's the brilliance, is no one's going to look that closely.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That's in your brain forever.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Literally forever.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All righty. Anybody else have anything to do before the evening's rest, or are you happy to continue the next day?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Ja. That is the best way to do that. For anyone.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Would magical items help further your abilities or blessings?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That seems like an abstract question. There are certainly very useful things that can be found in this world. You never know.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Are you okay?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I'm fine, I'm just fascinated by all things wizard and I want you to succeed, that's all. I have great faith in you, I know you'll do great things, and I just want to help you get there.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, thank you, friend.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Sure. Yes. Anything, anything to help the cause.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, maybe we go to bed and tomorrow we don't tear the roof down off of every building we go into again, yeah?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Sounds good.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: So I take out my silver thread and I start casting the Alarm spell on our room before bed, and then I go to bed.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All right, so, as the evening comes to a close, the next morning greets you on a somewhat chilled, misty morning, the sky gray, but the light is out. You can see a faint mist is burning off of the streets in the early morning, like six or seven-ish. You all gather yourselves. Is there anything you wish to accomplish during the day, or do you wish to jump ahead to the meeting of the afternoon, the evening?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Are you okay?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I'm fine, I'm just fascinated by all things wizard and I want you to succeed, that's all. I have great faith in you, I know you'll do great things, and I just want to help you get there.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, thank you, friend.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Sure. Yes. Anything, anything to help the cause.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, maybe we go to bed and tomorrow we don't tear the roof down off of every building we go into again, yeah?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Sounds good.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: So I take out my silver thread and I start casting the Alarm spell on our room before bed, and then I go to bed.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All righty. Anybody else have anything to do before the evening's rest, or are you happy to continue the next day?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Next day for me.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (shouting) Pillow fight!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Beau gets a little too aggressive with the pillow fight.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Ow, okay. We're done.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Sorry, sorry.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Mess with the bull, get the horns.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: I mean, the harvest is celebrated at different points, and in a few weeks is the official Harvest End festival. This is done in advance, it's more of a private event that is for the more elite. Some folks come in from out of town, from local smaller townships that are pretty well off and trying to rub elbows with the local elite. It's a festival that contains food, music, drink. You've never been to one, of course, and you've heard of it from the time you spent in Zadash. You've never seen it, but you've seen folks take care through the street towards the event, and it seems to be fairly like a more subdued Mardi Gras colors, but the colors are more reds and browns and golds. They have masks they'll wear for it, but they aren't full face, just habitual eye masks. Only some will, some folks don't get fully into the spirit.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Should we get some awesome all-black clothes?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Maybe we should so that we stealth better.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, something a little bit more stealthy.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We can put it in the pink bag.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I like that.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I was thinking some carnival masks, from the other direction.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, all black looks like you're up to no good.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, and if you are actually in the dark, you can't tell the difference, and if you're in the light you look like a ninja, so I think we're good, maybe.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I see what you're saying. Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Everyone’s going to be pretty fancy-dressed.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: The carnival is going on today?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, the festival is happening this evening.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Do we know anything about this festival, like, culturally? Do they wear masks?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: I'll say, because of your dress, I'll give you advantage on this.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: A lot is riding on Sutan and the High-Richter going to this festival.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That's it, that's all. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I was really expecting more there.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: No, it's just pessimism.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Let's go buy some masks, then.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Are you afraid they're not party people?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, I worry that if we break into Sutan's house and stuff a bunch of letters there, but the High-Richter stays in with a tummy ache, then it all falls apart.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Well, then we go from there.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: There's no point in worrying about things you can't change yet, Caleb.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: She's got a point.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Be the chaos you want to see in the world.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, no point in worrying, it only makes it hurt twice.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, lead the way, I'll be the caboose.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. Who’s helming the expedition to acquire costume pieces?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Molly.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Molly, go ahead and make an investigation check. (laughter)

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: 11. It takes you the better part of the morning. About noon, you manage to find a place that will sell similar outfits. Since a lot of them are either custom-made for the individuals that are going, it's hard to find a place. There is no shop that sells fancy clothes to the random folks who aren't going to the event, but you manage to find a few individuals that can cobble together a few masks and basic attire. Because it's a quick build, it's going to be a little pricier than normal cost, so the commission will take the better part of the afternoon, and will be ready by sundown for enough outfits for six of you. Or accoutrements.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I was really expecting more there.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: No, it's just pessimism.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Let's go buy some masks, then.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Are you afraid they're not party people?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, I worry that if we break into Sutan's house and stuff a bunch of letters there, but the High-Richter stays in with a tummy ache, then it all falls apart.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Well, then we go from there.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: There's no point in worrying about things you can't change yet, Caleb.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: She's got a point.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Be the chaos you want to see in the world.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, no point in worrying, it only makes it hurt twice.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, lead the way, I'll be the caboose.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. Who’s helming the expedition to acquire costume pieces?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Molly.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Molly, go ahead and make an investigation check. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I'll say, because of your dress, I'll give you advantage on this.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Thank you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Show us where Melrose is.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Looking for Melrose. 11.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You gather the outfits that were commissioned for you. You have your materials. As the day grows later, eventually the sun begins to crest over the western mountain range. The sky, though cloudy still, the colors begin to shift to the oranges and purples of dusk. You begin to see carriages come through the streets and make their way towards the Tri-Spire district.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, the last thing we want is for the crownsguard to think that this was all us.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Where are we meeting Ulog?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: In front of the--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Song and Supper Inn.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh also, Frumpkin is up here in my sort of scarf position, and I bring him down in my lap, and I take the sausages out, and I sort of like knead one of the sausages in my hand a bit, and I say, you know, I'm sorry, but the next time that you go off to your pocket place and come back, this will be gone. And I start squishing sausage grease into his hair, and rubbing it down his back so that he smells like sausage.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That's a very good plan.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I love you very much, so thank you for this, okay?

preceding_turns.speaker: ALL
preceding_turns.text: (angry cat noises)

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Do you want to eat him even more now?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Some other time, perhaps.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'll drink instead.

payload.trigger_phrase: you make your way to the Song
payload.raw_text: You, however, you make your way to the Song and Supper, and on the inside, you can see, nervously at a table, in a dark cloak, Ulog. "What the--?" Gets up and meets you outside, shoves you back out there and you both begin to walk side-by-side. "I was waiting here long enough!"

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns_last5.text: We’re going to sit in the sewer like so much clown.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns_last5.text: We recapped this at the break, too! Damn it!

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: You guys are sitting there, waiting for Jester to show up and meet you to let you out.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: I cast Message a few times. We’re here. Where are you?

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns_last5.text: Where letter-R letter-U?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: He looks down and he has fairly plain dark clothing for night-stealthing. He has like a single-- looks like a gold chain of some kind with a single yellowish amber-orange bead on it that looks like it’s worth a gold or two. Dark green-brown cloak. It’s hard to see the color, this late at night. He doesn’t look super rich.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We’re going to sit in the sewer like so much clown.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We recapped this at the break, too! Damn it!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You guys are sitting there, waiting for Jester to show up and meet you to let you out.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I cast Message a few times. We’re here. Where are you?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Where letter-R letter-U?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You, however, you make your way to the Song and Supper, and on the inside, you can see, nervously at a table, in a dark cloak, Ulog. "What the--?" Gets up and meets you outside, shoves you back out there and you both begin to walk side-by-side. "I was waiting here long enough!"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We were waiting for you! It’s so weird that we totally missed you, though.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a deception check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, well, well, 11.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He goes, "Right. Well, where’s the rest of your team?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: They are-- Here’s the thing. How rich do you look? How rich does he look?

payload.trigger_phrase: You make your way to the gate
payload.raw_text: You make your way to the gate once more. The guards there-- One of them walks up and, the same one from earlier, gives you a nod. "Hold."

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: You put some of the festive wear on him. It’s all not at all fitted for him and it looks like a kid who has outgrown his snow-outfit and he’s like-- "This is humiliating."

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns_last5.text: It’ll get you in the door, don’t worry. Here, put this mask on.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: He’s seething beneath it. You can see him grinding his teeth, but he follows you nevertheless.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns_last5.text: Candy-themed, too.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns_last5.text: Oh, that’s great!

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You also hear the music begin to swell at the various performing bands towards the center where this gala is. You can see all the banners leaning in the center space. The sunset colors have vanished and now the night sky holds above. The Crownsguard are clustered in greater numbers in the Tri-Spire, specifically to keep a perimeter around the event. As you glance off to the entrance gate where you were, you can see there’s now stationed more towards that space, since you originally passed through. More musicians are arriving, some locally, some appear to be from other areas within the actual Empire. The social elite are gathering in the center of the Silken Terrace and you can now see the small carriages are stopping and letting other individuals out to join the crowds, the mingling beginning. You can already see about 60 or 70 people, with more still arriving. They all have their various colors and masks on. They’re all grabbing drinks and laughing and seeing old friends and introducing themselves to new people they haven’t met before. Some of them live in the Tri-Spire, some that live throughout and beyond.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: D&D Beyond!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: And Far Cry 5!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Far Cry 5! It’s five better than Far Cry 0.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Or four better than the original.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: The fiviest.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Takes place in America.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Montana.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Guys, it’s Larkin.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Well there goes the campaign. As you guys make your way through the city streets, you can see the closer you get to the Silken Terrace, for one, the usual silk banners that adorn the various edges of the streets have been changed from the usual white silk banners to bright yellows, bright browns, bright reds. The colors of autumn are now starting to show and appear throughout the vicinity.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I hate the color yellow.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Really? Why?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I just hate it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It would look really pretty on you.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I don’t think that’s true.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: No, I don’t think so.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I think yellow would look good on you.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: The blue works. Stick with it, you know.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: More of a spring…

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Okay. And leap. Make an athletics check. I'll give you advantage because of the step of the wind.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: She's going to try and catch it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I rolled a 19. I don't know what I'm adding.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. That's fine. So you manage to just catch it, holding onto the edge of the rope, before it clanks onto the ground. It's just dangling now.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh my god.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You pull it slowly back up.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Wait.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You said strength. I threw it as hard as I could throw it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Quiet, shut up!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Thunder!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: (whispers) Okay, maybe if I get over, I could hold the other end and help people.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You can jump ten feet?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I think I can.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, dear lord. What if you fall on the ground, Beau?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Let this happen. Let this happen.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Look, we're basically gods, okay? (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I can make this jump.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You going to try?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to cast step of the wind-- Sorry, not cast it. I'm going to use a ki point. I'm going to take a breath, gather my chi. Along the edge of the branch anime run--

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: We'll say it takes you about five minutes or so to finally get all those off carefully. You manage to pry it open, and it opens up ever so slightly, and there's a chain on each end that prevent it from (slamming open). It opens up just enough that you can slip inside, and there is a small ladder there that you can use to slowly get down into a small attic.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: No, get them. Get them.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Let's go down the chimney. At least I can go down there and look to see if it's possible.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There is a very faint trail of smoke coming out of the chimney, by the way.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: There's a fire in there. What if we pull up these shingles?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, pull up the shingles.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: And, like, stack them.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So you begin to collect them. Where are you putting them?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: In the haversack!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. So you take a moment to go ahead and carefully-- You have a collection now of roughly 23 roof shingles in your haversack.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Those could be valuable.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So you can add that to your equipment.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Are they clay shingles? Very valuable.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Look out, giant red dragons.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. (laughs)

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Okay, the carpet peels back and it reveals beneath it a section of the wood that has a hinge bolted to one side of it. It's a square that's cut out of it, it looks like it's an open case. The carpet peels back, and then begins to fold back in its space, and then fold up again. It begins to slightly shift and rise, and the carpet almost seems to be gliding in the air on its own in the vicinity. It spins towards you and then rushes in your direction. I need everyone to roll initiative.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Right. The carpet seems magical to me, I wouldn't think that something under the carpet is magical.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yep, I saw it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Natural 20.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Natural 20! As they're having this conversation, you glance by the fireplace, the fire is crackling in there, and you glance down and right underneath the hearth, up where the fire's tickling, you see a piece of the stonework that doesn't match the others, it's slightly off-colored. It looks like the burning of the fire that's been given this dark and blackened look to it, there's one brick there that doesn't seem to have been affected by any of the soot at all.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I, avoiding the rug, lightly step over it, and see if I can jiggy the brick out.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As your finger presses against the brick it slides in about a half an inch, and you hear this (click) sound over by where the metal large cabinet is.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You just unlocked the fucking metal shit!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh shit!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Do I see any change in the abjuration magic when she does that?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Does it go away?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It vanishes. (cheering)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to cast Message. We are the fucking best! You can respond to this message! (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I got to get that seal!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: What you just did dispelled whatever ward was on the cabinet, however the carpet I can't tell exactly what's going on but there is something--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Use the Mage Hand to move the carpet.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Give me the papers.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I cast Mage Hand and slowly peel back a corner of the carpet.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I hand the two letters that we made for Lord Sutan.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Yes, roll initiative, please. Beau, you're over by the fireplace so you've just seen that happen there, and I think that's--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You just unlocked the fucking metal shit!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh shit!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Do I see any change in the abjuration magic when she does that?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Does it go away?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It vanishes. (cheering)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to cast Message. We are the fucking best! You can respond to this message! (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I got to get that seal!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: What you just did dispelled whatever ward was on the cabinet, however the carpet I can't tell exactly what's going on but there is something--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Use the Mage Hand to move the carpet.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Give me the papers.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I cast Mage Hand and slowly peel back a corner of the carpet.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I hand the two letters that we made for Lord Sutan.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, the carpet peels back and it reveals beneath it a section of the wood that has a hinge bolted to one side of it. It's a square that's cut out of it, it looks like it's an open case. The carpet peels back, and then begins to fold back in its space, and then fold up again. It begins to slightly shift and rise, and the carpet almost seems to be gliding in the air on its own in the vicinity. It spins towards you and then rushes in your direction. I need everyone to roll initiative.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We're fighting a carpet! The fuck!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We're fighting a carpet.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We had it coming.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It's an animated object!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I had that in my one-shot.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: My kids have fought a broom. This happens.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Whoa, look at that carpet!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That carpet is now gone, because instead you are fighting that carpet. (cheering)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It's like a cobra ready to strike!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All righty, so Nott would've been over here.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: So we're rolling initiative, that's what we're doing?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: It's true! Nott, it's your turn first. You are restrained, you are blinded, and you are suffocating. At the start of your turn, you immediately take 12 points of bludgeoning damage as it's now crushing and constricting your entire body. You feel the life being pulled out of you as you're gasping for breath in this space. What are you going to do with your turn?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It gets a surprise round.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It does, because it suddenly rushes in your direction. That is going to be a 21 to hit.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: That hits.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Natural 16. It goes and wraps around you and completely envelops you within itself. All of a sudden you just see darkness, and you cannot breathe.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, this is going to suck.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Currently you are enveloped by the Rug of Smothering.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: The Rug of Smothering.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Initiative here. Top of the round, 25 to 20 anybody.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 24, that'll be you first, Nott. 20 to 15?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Beau. 15 to 10?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What'd you get Caleb?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Well you know, I rolled a nine.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh my god!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I’m not in this one, fucker. You guys are the best, that's the last we heard. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (singing) You're the best around! Nothing's ever going to bring you down!

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Yeah, you would have disadvantage because you're restrained, but it's also smothering you so I would give you advantage to that, so it's just a straight roll.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's true! Nott, it's your turn first. You are restrained, you are blinded, and you are suffocating. At the start of your turn, you immediately take 12 points of bludgeoning damage as it's now crushing and constricting your entire body. You feel the life being pulled out of you as you're gasping for breath in this space. What are you going to do with your turn?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: A VS spell is verbal. Can I talk?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes, you can still talk. You're (muffled sounds). You still have the breath you had in your body when it went around you, so I'll say you have enough air for one spell and then that's all.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay. I will touch it with Shocking Grasp, cast Shocking Grasp on it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Is that a spell attack roll?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It's touch, to hit plus five. So I have to roll.

payload.raw_text: There you go. Top of the round. Nott, your turn. As you come to consciousness, you are still prone.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: All righty. So how much does Nott heal?

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: 14 points.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns_last5.text: Whoa!

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: From one healing potion?

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: Greater.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: There you go. Top of the round. Nott, your turn. As you come to consciousness, you are still prone.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: What? It moves into the fireplace?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Well it would move her away. No, it's not going to do that.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: (screams)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It doesn't have enough movement to move much. That brings us to the bottom. Caleb, you're up.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I pull the potion of healing I have out of my belt, and I run over to Nott and I jam it into her mouth and pour it in. The whole time I'm going through my list of spells thinking of what could help. There's nothing, but I'm going: Dancing Lights, Fire Bolt, Friends, Alarm, Burning Hands, Chromatic Orb, Comprehend Languages, Detect Magic. And I heal Nott.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Go ahead and roll for the heal on that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: And before I got wrapped up I said it's not immune to any-- (choking) It has no weaknesses-- (choking)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All righty. So how much does Nott heal?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: 14 points.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: As you all slowly come to rest and look back, and Caleb, as you walk out, you stop at the landing next to Ulog, and Beau-- oh sorry, you're not there. You stop next to Ulog as, right there at the bottom of the stairs looking up, you see, in a very beautiful, elegant, dark green dress, the High-Richter Prucine. "Hmm. My, my. I'm glad I forgot my gift, or I would have missed you reprobates breaking and entering the home of a High-Richter. I believe that carries a life sentence in the Umber Dungeon, wouldn't you say? If I might ask, what are you looking for? You have my curiosity piqued." There's a brief pause, as you're all glancing down. Ulog holds up the paper in his hand, and he goes, "You. You put my wife in chains and you know she's innocent! We got proof now! It's over for you." She chuckles and scoffs as she falls onto her back leg. "And how do you plan to deliver it? Curious. You think my home won't be swarming with guards in a few minutes?" She pulls a brass bell out from under her pouch and rings it outside--
